mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and force the Federal Reserve to eventually tighten policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC of CPIAUCSL level on 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (absolute IC < 0.02) or the proportion of positive returns during rising CPI months exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below its 12-month moving average, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below its 12-month trailing moving average
failure_condition: In triggered months, the median 20-day forward return is not significantly greater than the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05) or the hit rate (positive direction) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in both housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate synchronized consumer demand weakness, which signals an impending economic slowdown and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month on a year-over-year adjusted basis
failure_condition: During triggered episodes, the median 60-day forward return is not below -1.5% or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 40%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress (identified by elevated industrial production volatility), a month-over-month decline in INDPRO exceeding 1% signals acute supply disruptions that depress corporate earnings and forward equity returns over the next week.
regime_hint: supply chain disruption regime (INDPRO volatility > its 75th percentile)
failure_condition: When triggered outside the supply chain stress regime, the 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the median return during stress regime triggers is above -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate (GDPC1) exceeding 3% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth rate exceeds 3%
failure_condition: In quarters where the trigger fires, the subsequent 60-day return is positive less than 60% of the time, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional average (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 4% while the Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2%, real interest rates turn deeply negative, fueling speculative asset bubbles but also creating eventual valuation corrections; over the 20-day horizon, this combination signals rising equity risk premiums and negative forward returns.
regime_hint: high inflation with low policy rate
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the median 20-day forward return is not negative, or the probability of a negative return is not above 55% in a statistically significant proportion of samples.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, supporting corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC of the monthly change in PAYEMS on 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (IC < 0.02), or the correlation breaks down in out-of-sample periods after 2021.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in unemployment (UNRATE) and declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal a feedback loop of weakening labor markets and collapsing residential investment, characteristic of recessionary dynamics that produce strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3% month-over-month and HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: When triggered, the median 60-day forward return is not below -3% or the proportion of negative returns is not above 65% with statistical significance (binomial test p < 0.05).
